strategy_summary: The back legs of a male platypus provide defense against predators and other males via poisonous spurs. Image: E.
mechanism:
The back legs of a male platypus provide defense against predators and other males via poisonous spurs.

Image: E. Lonnon / Wikimedia Commons / toggle icon Image: Pedro Lopez / toggle icon right arrow right arrow

“One last peculiarity of the platypus is the sharp horny spur on the back feet of the males, through which poison from a poison gland inside the leg can be injected with a slashing kick. The spur is used in defence and against other males in contests, and the poison is strong enough to be dangerous to humans — not that many of them come into contact with platypuses.” (Foy and Oxford Scientific Films 1982:182)
